-------------------

Welcome to our video on the top things to do in Hurghada, a beautiful resort town located on the Red Sea coast of Egypt. Hurghada is a popular tourist destination, known for its stunning beaches, crystal-clear waters, and world-class snorkeling and diving.
 
First on our list is a visit to the Giftun Islands, a group of beautiful uninhabited islands located just off the coast of Hurghada. The islands are a popular destination for snorkeling and diving, with vibrant coral reefs and a range of marine life, including tropical fish, dolphins, and sea turtles.
 
Next up is the Hurghada Marina, a picturesque waterfront promenade lined with restaurants, cafes, and shops. The marina is a great place to relax and soak up the atmosphere, or take a boat tour of the Red Sea.
 
For a taste of local culture, head to the El Dahar neighborhood, the historic heart of Hurghada. This area is home to a range of shops, markets, and mosques, as well as the Hurghada Museum, which showcases the history and culture of the Red Sea region.
 
Another must-see attraction is the Hurghada Grand Aquarium, a large marine park featuring a range of exhibits and activities. Visitors can explore the underwater world of the Red Sea, from the coral reefs to the deep sea, and learn about the marine life that inhabits this vibrant ecosystem.
 
For those seeking adventure, head to the Desert Safari, a thrilling off-road excursion into the surrounding desert. The safari includes a range of activities, such as camel riding, quad biking, and sandboarding, as well as a traditional Bedouin-style dinner under the stars. 

Last but not least, don't miss the chance to relax on Hurghada's beautiful beaches. The town has a range of beaches to choose from, including the popular Makadi Bay, Sahl Hasheesh, and El Gouna beaches. Spend a day soaking up the sun, swimming in the clear blue waters, or trying out some water sports like windsurfing or parasailing. 

There you have it, our top picks for the best things to do in Hurghada. Whether you're interested in snorkeling and diving, culture and history, or just relaxing on the beach, this town has something for everyone. So what are you waiting for? Start planning your trip today and discover all that Hurghada has to offer.

Besides being a place for walking and dining, the marina is the starting point for numerous sea excursions. From here, catamarans and boats set sail to destinations like the Giftton Islands or dolphin house, offering activities such as snorkeling, diving, and dolphin watching. At night, the area transforms into an entertainment hub with street performers, caricatururists, and live music. Number two, Orange Bay. [music] From Herata Marina, you can take a boat trip to Orange Bay, one of the most paradiscal destinations in the Red Sea. This corner of turquoise waters and white sands offers a perfect combination of relaxation, adventure, and natural beauty. [music] Its protected environment hosts pristine coral reefs and a rich marine biodiversity, making it an ideal spot for snorkeling, and other water activities. Upon arrival at Orange Bay, visitors are welcomed by wooden structures that blend harmoniously with the landscape. The beach is equipped with sun loungers, umbrellas, and rest areas that invite you to relax under the sun. [snorts] There are also swings and hammocks in the water, perfect for capturing memorable photos. Excursions to Orange Bay usually include stops for snorkeling at two different spots where you can see tropical fish and vibrant corals. Activities such as banana boat rides or inflatable sofas are also offered, adding a touch of fun and adrenaline to the day. [music] In the description, I'll leave the link so you can book the same tour we took. Number one, beaches. The beaches of Heratada are some of the most stunning on the Red Sea, offering a variety of landscapes and experiences for every taste. [music] One of the most popular is Makotti Bay Beach. Located about 30 minutes south of downtown Herata. It is known for its crystal clear water and calm seabeds, perfect for snorkeling. Here you can enjoy an excellent view while relaxing on the golden sand. Another must visit is Soma Bay Beach. Famous for its natural beauty and top-notch facilities. This beach is ideal for water sports enthusiasts such as wind surfing and kite surfing thanks to the constant winds in the area. If you're looking for a quieter, less touristy beach, I recommend Gaona Beach. This is one of the best options for those wanting to escape the bustle of the busier areas. Gona is a small resort town known for its lagoons, private islands, and relaxed atmosphere. It's perfect for swimming, snorkeling, or simply enjoying the view without the crowds. Mama Beach is another destination that stands out for its natural beauty. It is a small island just a short boat ride from Herada. The beach is peaceful and ideal for snorkeling as the nearby coral reefs are home to a wide variety of fish. Mahmia is one of the cleanest and most protected beaches, making it an excellent choice for those wanting to enjoy nature in its purest form. videos.title: Hurghada Egypt - 15 Things To See and Do | Egypt - Best Places To Visit #egypt
videos.description:
Join our channel and you can choose topics for next videos! https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCPptF5I-WTacVntxij40Mkg/join

Welcome to Hurghada, a fascinating coastal city full of enchantment and adventure around every corner. There is something for everyone, from taking a look at the beautiful aquatic life at the Hurghada Grand Aquarium to going on exhilarating desert safaris. Prepare to be amazed by sand sculptures, enjoy the sun at opulent beaches, and acquaint yourself with Egypt's rich history and culture. So let's explore this entire video and subscribe to this channel.🙏 https://www.youtube.com/@travelmaxhd

⏲️⏲️ Time Stamps ⏲️⏲️ 

00:00 Intro
00:25 Sand City Hurghada
01:11 Hurghada Grand Aquarium
01:50 Indulge in a Fun Desert Safari
02:37 Selkhet Papyrus Museum
03:13 Visit The Pyramids and Great Sphinx of Giza
03:53 Mini Egypt Park
04:39 Plan a Day Trip to the Historical Luxor
05:28 One of the Best Places in Egypt
06:12 Take a Boat Tour to Orange Bay
06:59 Take Your Kids to Dolphin World
07:37 Hurghada Museum
08:26 Glass Bottom Boat
09:13 Best Beaches in Hurghada
09:54 Visit Abu Simbel Temples
10:39 Mahmya Island for a Day Trip
11:27 Outro

Discover the breathtaking Sand City Hurghada, an outdoor museum that vividly depicts the alluring world of sand art. Admire the exquisite craftsmanship and limitless imagination displayed in the spectacular sand structures, which range from cartoon creatures to pyramids. Sand art has a history of more than 500 years and has spread throughout the world, sparking competitions and other events. 

Sand City, which is only 24 minutes south of Hurghada's center, has two fascinating exhibits: Historical Art and Wonderland Art. Discover the enchantment of 15 sand sculptures and 10 reliefs in the Wonderland Art area, as well as the 22 magnificent sand masterpieces and 5 reliefs of the Historical Art.

The Hurghada Grand Aquarium is a place where Red Sea marine life is displayed, conservation is encouraged, and tourists are educated. It is one of the biggest indoor aquatic environments in the world, with 24 galleries and many accolades. The aquarium also has exhibits on fossils, rainforests, and animal conservation in addition to marine life. 

Dive with sharks or take a 24-meter underwater tunnel tour for an authentic underwater adventure. Astonishing shark feedings can be seen as well. There is no need to make an appointment; you can purchase tickets at the door. Want some exhilarating adventures in Hurghada? Prepare yourself for an exhilarating desert adventure safari! On a quad or jeep excursion, rumble across the captivating desert vistas while taking in the spectacular environment. Take part in a tour that is packed with variety and excitement. 

Under the boundless expanse of Egyptian blue skies, enjoy the thrill of a powered buggy ride or gently travel the dunes on a camel. Tea refreshments and light snacks can be had throughout your tour to ensure continuous desert exploration. You can customize your journey to focus on one big desert activity or combine several thrills in a single day by selecting from half-day to full-day safaris.

Learn about the history and creation of papyrus by exploring the greatest ancient collection. Explore the largest papyrus exhibition in Egypt to see the wonderful artwork produced on this ancient material. Watch as experts demonstrate the production of papyrus and some of its applications in a live workshop. One of the first types of paper manufactured from plant resources is papyrus, which dates back to 2900 B.C. Learn about the creation and significance of this ancient writing system by immersing yourself in its universe.

Get ready to be mesmerized by sand art, soak up the sun at luxurious beaches, and immerse yourself in the rich history and culture of Egypt. Number 15. Be Blown Away by the Sand Art at Sand City Hurghada. Experience the awe-inspiring Sand City Hurghada, an open-air museum that brings the captivating world of sand art to life. Admire the intricate details and boundless creativity showcased in the magnificent sand structures, from pyramids to cartoon characters. With a history spanning 500 years, sand art has become a global phenomenon, inspiring competitions and activities worldwide. Located just 24 minutes south of downtown Hurghada, Sand City offers two captivating exhibits: Historical Art and Wonderland Art. Explore the 22 stunning sand creations and 5 reliefs of the Historical Art, and delve into the wonder of 15 sand sculptures and 10 reliefs in the Wonderland Art section. Number 14. Learn About Egypt’s Marine Life at Hurghada Grand Aquarium. The Hurghada Grand Aquarium is a space devoted to showcasing Red Sea sea life, promoting conservation, and educating visitors. With 24 galleries and numerous awards, it is one of the largest indoor aquatic habitats globally. Beyond marine life, the aquarium features exhibits on rainforests, fossils, and animal conservation. Experience an immersive under-the-sea journey in the 24-meter underwater tunnel or dive with sharks. Witness thrilling shark feedings as well. No appointment is necessary; tickets can be bought at the door. Number 13. Indulge in a Fun Desert Safari. Craving thrilling escapades in Hurghada? Get ready for an adrenaline-pumping desert adventure safari! Roar across the mesmerizing desert landscapes on a quad or jeep tour, soaking in the breathtaking scenery. Immerse yourself in a tour that's brimming with excitement and diversity. Experience the thrill of a motorized buggy ride, or gracefully traverse the dunes on a camel, beneath the endless expanse of Egyptian blue skies. Recharge during your expedition with refreshing tea and light snacks, ensuring non-stop desert exploration. Choose from half-day to full-day safaris, tailoring your adventure to focus on one epic desert activity or combine multiple thrills in a single day. Number 12. Learn About the Earliest Form of Paper at the Selkhet Papyrus Museum. Explore the largest ancient papyrus collection to learn about its history and production. Discover the fascinating art created on this ancient paper in Egypt's largest papyrus gallery. Witness a live workshop where professionals demonstrate the process of making papyrus and its various uses. Papyrus, dating back to 2900 B.C., is one of the earliest forms of paper made from plant materials. Immerse yourself in the world of this ancient writing medium and gain insights into its invention and significance. Number 11. Visit The Pyramids and Great Sphinx of Giza. Embark on an unforgettable adventure to the Pyramids and Great Sphinx of Giza. Step into the ancient world, where colossal wonders rise from the golden sands. As you wander amidst the mystic ruins, the air whispers tales of pharaohs and forgotten civilizations. Gaze upon the majestic Pyramids, marvel at their architectural brilliance, and ponder the secrets they guard. And there, standing proud and enigmatic, behold the Great Sphinx, a timeless guardian of the desert. Let history unravel before your eyes, immersing you in a journey beyond imagination. Number 10. Don’t Miss Your Chance to Tower of Egypt’s Monuments at Mini Egypt Park. xplore the miniature museum of Egypt, featuring sculptural pieces by over 50 artists depicting traditional Egyptian monuments in small versions. Marvel at miniatures of iconic landmarks like the Roman Amphitheater, The New Theater of Alexandria, El Montazah Palace, and The Cairo Tower. Capture unique photos, such as holding the top of an Egyptian pyramid or creating fun illusions with emblematic structures. Despite their small size, this museum is a favorite among visitors of all ages, including children. If you're short on time, a visit to Mini Egypt Park allows you to experience these sights in person, right away. Number 9. Plan a Day Trip to the Historical Luxor. Luxor, previously the ancient capital city of Thebes, is a 4-hour drive south of Hurghada. It now stands as an open-air museum, with two major sights: Luxor Temple and Karnak Temple. Explore these towering Egyptian structures and discover the ancient temples and tombs that still exist. Karnak Temple, standing for over 2,000 years, is the second-largest temple complex globally. Luxor Temple, dating back to 1400 BC, is believed to have been a coronation site for royalty. Each temple has its own museum, displaying excavated artifacts and offering intriguing insights into their history. Across the Nile lies the Valley of the Kings, where approximately 60 tombs serve as the final resting place for Thebes' royalty. Number 8. Enjoy Scuba Diving in One of the Best Places in Egypt. Don't miss scuba diving in Hurghada, with Gota Abu Ramada as a top diving site just an hour south of the city. Explore a stunning coral garden and learn about preserving Red Sea reefs. Witness diverse marine life like butterflyfish, surgeonfish, eels, and possibly sharks. Visit Careless Reef, known for its varied coral species, including vibrant soft and hard corals. Opt for a scuba diving excursion with a professional guide for the best experience. Choose from full or half-day options, including lunch. Dive into unforgettable underwater adventures while being expertly guided. Number 7. Take a Boat Tour to Orange Bay. Embark on a breathtaking boat tour to Orange Bay on Giftun Island. Enjoy beach swings, hammocks, and a range of water activities like snorkeling and swimming. Marvel at the scenic views of Hurghada's coast while sailing across the Red Sea. Discover vibrant coral reefs and diverse marine life on immersive underwater tours. It's an ideal spot for a safe and enjoyable beach day with children, offering calm waters and soft sand. Easily reach the island with a 20-minute boat ride from Hurghada Marina. Some tours even include lunch, swimming with dolphins, and other exciting aquatic experiences. Experience the beauty of Orange Bay and create lasting memories. Number 6. Take Your Kids to Dolphin World. Experience the awe-inspiring show at Dolphin World, where families can be amazed by the intelligence, talent, and interaction of dolphins. Watch as the dolphins sing, dance, juggle, and communicate right before your eyes, delighting children and adults alike. Dolphin World offers a range of activities, including attending the show, swimming with the dolphins, and taking photos with them. Tickets can be purchased online per activity. Immerse yourself in the captivating world of dolphins and create unforgettable memories with your family. Number 5. Enter the World of Pharaohs at the Hurghada Museum. Discover the captivating history of Egypt at the beautiful Hurghada Museum. Immerse yourself in exhibits featuring ornate art, historical objects, and ancient Egyptian tools, providing a fascinating educational experience. The museum's mission is to showcase Egypt's history, culture, tradition, and beauty throughout different eras. Explore the five eras of Egypt, from Ancient Egypt to Greco-Roman and modern times, gaining insights into the city's foundation. Marvel at replicas of Pharaohs' tombs and a pharaonic cemetery, delving into the intricate details of this intriguing history. Conveniently located between the airport and the heart of Hurghada, the museum is easily accessible for solo travelers and families alike. Number 4. Go on a trip to the Red sea with a glass bottom boat. Set sail on an enchanting escapade across the Red Sea, where the ordinary becomes extraordinary. Brace yourself for a remarkable journey aboard a glass bottom boat, your window into an aquatic wonderland. As the vessel glides gently over the azure waters, immerse yourself in a surreal spectacle of vibrant coral gardens and elusive sea creatures. Behold the delicate dance of technicolor fish, the playful antics of dolphins, and the majesty of graceful manta rays. Feel the thrill of floating above this mesmerizing marine tapestry, a magical portal to a hidden realm. Prepare for an unparalleled adventure as you embark on a trip that defies imagination. Number 3. Spend Some Time at the Best Beaches in Hurghada. Discover luxurious beach experiences at Sunrise Garden Beach Resort, near seaside restaurants, spas, and shopping. Hurghada Marriott Beach offers a fun-filled day with beach lounging, activities, and beach club nightlife. Coral Beach Hurghada is perfect for snorkeling, scuba diving, or sipping iced drinks by the beach. Public Beach Number 9 welcomes locals and visitors for a great day, with snacks, beach views, and aquatic activities from 8 am – 8 pm. Enjoy tranquility at Old Vic Beach in Hurghada, with beach chair rentals, beachside lunches, and serene Red Sea dips. Number 2. Visit Abu Simbel Temples. Enter a realm of ancient grandeur as you journey to the legendary Abu Simbel Temples. Behold the colossal façade rising from the Nubian desert, defying the sands of time. Stand in reverence before the sun's rays penetrating the inner sanctum on two special days of the year, illuminating the gods with divine precision. This pilgrimage to Abu Simbel is an invitation to witness the enduring power of human ingenuity and the eternal quest for immortality. Number 1. Make Your Way to Mahmya Island for a Day Trip. Explore Giftun Island National Park for more snorkeling and diving opportunities, along with shaded beach spots to relax. Witness vibrant marine life and breathtaking island vistas. Avoid ice in your drinks too, because it's often made from tap water. Stick to the cheap bottled water for everything, even brushing your teeth. The water here isn't treated the same way as in places like Europe or the US. Shopping in Egypt can be a great experience, but make sure you know the prices. T-shirts usually cost around 270 to 360 Egyptian pounds, which is about 8 to 11 US dollar or 10 euros. Men can wear shorts too, no problem. You'll see security checkpoints at big tourist places like the pyramids, museums and even some hotels in Egypt. These checkpoints are there to keep tourists safe. Guards check bags and sometimes ask for your ID. The document who enters and makes sure nothing dangerous gets inside. These checks help prevent problems and keep the area secure for everyone. It's all about safety, so just have your ID ready and follow the instructions. It's quick and it's there to make sure you have a safe visit. Don't try to save a few euros or dollars and then ruin your vacation. When booking tours always go through your hotel or a reputable provider. Street vendors might offer cheaper deals, but they often come with risks like old unsafe boats or unprofessional guides. Booking through your hotel may cost a bit more, but it's worth it for the safety and peace of mind. Bargaining is a part of the shopping culture in Egypt.
